source_metadata.title_raw: Elizabeth M. Calciano - A-18-152 - August 15, 2018 - Burbank

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Elizabeth M. Calciano 
Assistant City Attorney 
City of Chino Hills 
2600 W. Olive Avenue, Suite 500 
Burbank, CA 91505 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-18-152 
Dear Ms. Calciano: 
August 15, 2018 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Chino Hills City Councilmember 
Brian Johsz regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1 and 
Section 1090. In addition, please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re 
Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and 
accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should 
contact us for additional advice. 
Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all 
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Los Angeles County 
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.l(c)(3).) We did not receive a 
written response from either entity. (Section 1097. l(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, 
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding 
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097 .1 ( c )( 5).) 
QUESTION 
1. Under Section 1090 and the Act, may Councilmember Johsz participate in and vote on a 
City contract for street-sweeping services with Arakelian Enterprises, Inc. (dba Athens 
Services) ("Athens"), given that he is also employed by Athens? 
2. May the City execute the contract with Athens in light of Councilmember Johsz's 
conflict of interest? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


CONCLUSION 
File No. A-18-152 
Page No. 2 
1. No. Councilmember Johsz may not make, participate in making, or use his position to 
influence any decisions regarding a contract with Athens due to his financial interest in 
his employer. 
2. Yes. Under Section 1091(b)(2), Councilmember Johsz's interest is remote and, so long 
as he abstains from making or participating in the making of the City's contract with 
Athens, the City Council may execute the contract. 
FACTS AS PROVIDED BY THE REQUESTOR 
In April 2015, the City of Chino Hills issued a request for proposal for contractors to 
provide street sweeping services. As a result, the City Council approved a three-year agreement 
with Athens in June 2015. The agreement expired on June 30, 2018, but allowed for two additional 
one-year extensions. 
In June 2017, the City Council approved an amendment to reduce Athens' services from 
four times per month to two times per month to reduce costs to the street sweeping special 
assessment district. The amendment provided for a total annual cost not-to-exceed $181,900. Due to 
an ongoing lack of appropriate funding in the special district, the City Council would like to 
consider reducing Athens' services to once a month in certain areas. Athens has requested a 3 .624-
percent increase for the potential one-year extension to their agreement, which will result in a total 
annual cost not-to-exceed $168,400. 
Councilmember Johsz is employed by Athens as a Director of Government Affairs and 
began working for Athens on September 8, 2014. Councilmember Johsz is not an officer of Athens, 
he does not own stock in Athens, he does not serve as a member of the board of directors, and he 
has not played any role in formulating the proposed contract amendment at Athens. Additionally, 
Councilmember Johsz stated that he has not spoken to anyone at the City regarding the contract 
amendment. Athens employs more than ten employees. 
Councilmember Johsz was appointed to the City Council on August 14, 2017, and began 
serving only after he was sworn into office on September 11, 2017. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 1090 
Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is the making of a contract in which the official has 
a financial interest." (People v. Honig (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 289, 333.) A contract that violates 
Section 1090 is void. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646.) Section 1090 is concerned with 
financial interests, other than remote or noninterests, that prevent public officials from exercising 
absolute loyalty and undivided allegiance in furthering the best interests of their agencies. (Stigall v. 
Taft (1962) 58 Cal.2d 565, 569.) An officer is conclusively presumed to be involved in the making 
of his or her agency's contracts when the officer is a member of a board or commission that has the 


File No. A-18-152 
Page No. 3 
power to execute the contract at issue. (Thomson, supra, p. 649.) As a general rule, when Section 
1090 is applicable to one member of a governing body of a public entity, the entire governing body 
is precluded from entering into the contract. (Stigall, supra, at p. 569.) 
This matter concerns a contract between the City and Athens, the employer of 
Councilmember Johsz. Under Section 1090, employees have been found to have a financial interest 
in a contract that involves their employer, even where the contract would not result in a change in 
income or directly involve the employee, because an employee has an overall interest in the 
financial success of the firm and continued employment. (84 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 158, 161-162 
(2001).), Therefore, because he is a member of the City Council, which has the power to execute 
City contracts for Athens' services, Section 1090 prohibits Councilmember Johsz and the City 
Council from making or participating in the making of the contract with Athens unless a remote or 
noninterest exception applies. 
Remote Interest 
The Legislature has created various statutory exceptions to Section 1090's prohibition where 
the financial interest involved is deemed to be a "remote interest," as defined in Section 1091. 
Specific to the provided facts, Section 1091 (b )(2) establishes that a "remote interest" exists where: 
(1) the private contracting party has ten or more employees other than the officer; (2) the officer 
was employed by the private contracting party at least three years prior to initially joining the public 
body; (3) the officer owns less than three-percent of the stock in the private contracting party; (4) 
the officer is not an officer or director of the private contracting party; and (5) the officer did not 
directly participate in formulating the bid of the private contracting party. 
Based on the facts provided, Athens employs more than ten employees. Councilmember 
Johsz is not an officer of Athens, he does not own stock in Athens, and did not directly participate 
in formulating the contract with Athens. Although Councilmember Johsz was appointed to City 
Council in August 2017, he was not sworn into office until September 11, 2017, and he indicated 
that he did not begin performing his duties until he was sworn in. Moreover, he began his 
employment at Athens three years prior to being sworn into office. Therefore, Councilmember 
Johsz has a remote interest in the City's contract for Athens' services under Section 109l(b)(2). 
When a "remote interest" is present, a board may execute a contract: (1) the officer in 
question discloses his or her financial interest in the contract to the public agency, (2) such interest 
is noted in the entity's official records, and (3) the officer abstains from any participation in the 
making of the contract. (Section 109l(a); 83 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen. 246,248 (2000).) Therefore, the 
City Council may enter into the contract with Athens so long as Councilmember Johsz discloses his 
financial interest in the contract, that disclosure is noted in the official records, and he abstains from 
any participation in making the contract. 
Political Reform Act 
Additionally, since the remedy in this situation is for Councilmember Johsz to abstain from 
any participation in the approval of the contract due to his remote interest, we do not need to further 
analyze a conflict of interest under the Act because abstention under Section 1091(a) also satisfies 


File No. A-18-152 
Page No. 4 
the Act's recusal requirements. However, we caution that under the Act the councilmember must 
leave the room if a decision regarding the contract is considered during a City Council Meeting. 
(Regulation 18707.) 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
SKP:jgl 
Sincerely, 
BrianG. Lau 
Acting General C 
Counsel, Legal Division
